text.title: LB High Availability Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-high-availability methodology end-to-end.

step.name: capture-sla
step.action: Capture target (99.9 / 99.95 / 99.99 / 99.999). Map customers to required regions. Decide single vs multi region.

step.name: failure-domain-analysis
step.input: Current or proposed topology + SLA tier.
step.action: Enumerate failure domains (process, host, AZ, region, DNS provider). For each: "if X fails, what happens?" Reject any path where one failure takes the whole service down.
step.decision-gate: If any SPOF is found in a tier targeting ≥ 99.9, STOP and redesign that tier.

step.name: pick-topology
step.action: Apply 06-decision-tree.xml. 99.9 single-region → 2 LB + 2 AZ + 2 backends/AZ. 99.99 multi-region → anycast GSLB + per-region 2-AZ replicas. 99.999 → multi-cloud anycast + drained read paths.
step.output: Topology block: LB instances, AZ count, GSLB kind.

step.name: wire-zero-downtime
step.action: Add SIGTERM handler flipping readiness to 503. Set drain delay ≥ 30 s (LB target group + K8s `terminationGracePeriodSeconds`). Roll deploys one AZ at a time. Confirm canary stage by killing one backend mid-traffic and observing zero 5xx.

step.name: record-and-validate
step.action: Render `templates/ha-decision-record.md` capturing SLA, topology, failure modes, mitigations. Run `validate-lb-high-availability.py --file artefact.json`. Schedule quarterly chaos test of LB failover + region failover.
